queue_item.action: Model peer-trigger lifecycle so request, scheduler acceptance, and verified peer execution cannot be conflated.

progress.finding: Peer-trigger request, scheduler acceptance, and peer execution are separate occurrences. Only a causally linked executed event carrying concrete execution evidence may prove execution.
progress.verification: 6 deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

queue_item.handoff: All teams should emit requested and accepted events when trigger tooling acknowledges scheduling, but report peer execution only after receiving a causally linked executed event containing the peer queue item and concrete commit, test, runtime, or deployment evidence.

reason: Shared queue state is updated through an append-only owned queue-update record to avoid overwriting other teams.
